message:
feat(config,browser): gate adapter-based I/O, encoding, and decoding families behind browser-IO subprofile split

Introduce the ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO compile-time capability macro and use
it to conditionally exclude the adapter-based public I/O family (bytes/
io_adapter, encoding pipeline, decoding pipeline) from minimal browser
builds, mirroring the existing browser-trace subprofile split pattern.

Config layer (asx_config.h):
- Define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO: evaluates to 0 when both ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER
  and ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL are defined, 1 otherwise. This gives
  minimal browser builds a smaller public API surface by excluding I/O
  adapter abstractions that depend on native byte-stream semantics.
- Define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO_SUBPROFILE_SPLIT = 1 as a contract sentinel
  so downstream code and tests can verify the split is active.
- Add explicit compile-time capability macros for features that are not
  yet implemented but whose absence was previously only documented in
  prose: ASX_HAS_TLS_NATIVE_ROOTS, ASX_HAS_TLS_WEBPKI_ROOTS,
  ASX_HAS_TLS_ROOT_VARIANTS (all 0), ASX_HAS_DB_SQLITE_BACKEND,
  ASX_HAS_DB_POSTGRES_BACKEND, ASX_HAS_DB_MYSQL_BACKEND,
  ASX_HAS_DB_BACKEND_VARIANTS (all 0), ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_KAFKA_BACKEND,
  ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_BACKEND_VARIANTS (all 0). These make the build
  contract machine-queryable rather than relying on docs alone.

Umbrella header (asx.h):
- Gate #include of bytes/io_adapter.h, encoding/encoding.h, and
  decoding/decoding.h behind the new ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO check so
  minimal browser builds never expose those families.

Self-gating headers (io_adapter.h, encoding.h, decoding.h):
- Each header now includes asx_config.h and wraps its public API
  declarations in #if !defined(ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER) || ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO
  guards, so direct inclusion in minimal browser code compiles to an
  empty translation unit rather than declaring unavailable symbols.

Source files (io_adapter.c, encoding.c, decoding.c):
- Wrap entire implementation bodies in matching #if guards so the
  object files are empty under minimal browser builds, avoiding
  link-time symbol pollution.

Test files (test_io_adapter.c, test_decoding.c, test_encoding.c):
- Wrap all existing test cases in the same #if guards.
- Add an #else fallback test in each suite that asserts the compile-time
  contract (ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO == 0 and ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO_SUBPROFILE_SPLIT == 1)
  so the test binary always produces meaningful output regardless of profile.

Browser boundary test (test_browser_boundary.c):
- Expand the compile_time_profile_contract test to assert all new
  capability macros: ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO_SUBPROFILE_SPLIT, the TLS root
  variants, the DB backend variants, and the messaging backend variants.
- Add minimal-vs-extended browser subprofile assertions for ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO
  alongside the existing ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE checks.
- Assert ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO == 1 in the non-browser (native) branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,6 +93,29 @@
 #define ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE 1
 #endif
 
+/* Browser I/O adapters/pipelines are only present in the extended browser
+ * subprofile. Minimal browser builds omit the public adapter-based IO,
+ * encoding, and decoding families from the umbrella surface. */
+#if defined(ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER) && defined(ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL)
+#define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO 0
+#else
+#define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO 1
+#endif
+#define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO_SUBPROFILE_SPLIT 1
+
+/* Backend-/variant-specific network subfeatures are not implemented yet.
+ * Keep these explicit so the public build contract can represent the current
+ * state without leaving feature absence implicit in docs alone. */
+#define ASX_HAS_TLS_NATIVE_ROOTS 0
+#define ASX_HAS_TLS_WEBPKI_ROOTS 0
+#define ASX_HAS_TLS_ROOT_VARIANTS 0
+#define ASX_HAS_DB_SQLITE_BACKEND 0
+#define ASX_HAS_DB_POSTGRES_BACKEND 0
+#define ASX_HAS_DB_MYSQL_BACKEND 0
+#define ASX_HAS_DB_BACKEND_VARIANTS 0
+#define ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_KAFKA_BACKEND 0
+#define ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_BACKEND_VARIANTS 0
+
 /* Browser trace is only present in the extended browser subprofile.
  * Minimal browser builds omit the public trace/lab/replay family from
  * the umbrella surface while preserving the existing default browser lane. */
